headline: United Airlines Launches New Summer Flights to Split, Bari, Glasgow and Santiago De Compostela
summary: This week, United Airlines will kick off its summer schedule with service to new European destinations in Croatia, Italy, Scotland and Spain. The new routes begin launching April 30 — giving customers even more options to reach unique, culturally rich destinations across Europe.

content.title: United Airlines Launches New Summer Flights to Split, Bari, Glasgow and Santiago De Compostela
content.summary: This week, United Airlines will kick off its summer schedule with service to new European destinations in Croatia, Italy, Scotland and Spain. The new routes begin launching April 30 — giving customers even more options to reach unique, culturally rich destinations across Europe.

thumbnail.caption: United launches flights to new cities in Croatia, Italy, Scotland and Spain for summer 2026.

headline: The DHS Shutdown Hits Day 73. Nightmare Airport Lines Could Return Soon.
summary: Travelers could again face longer lines at airport security checkpoints next month if Congress doesn’t figure out a way to fund the Department of Homeland Security this week.  The federal government is on track to run out of emergency funds used to pay some DHS workers during the past month of the department’s 73-day shutdown, including Transportation Security Administration employees that staff airport screening lines.  President Donald Trump had allocated $10 billion in emergency funds for DHS employees in executive orders in March and April.

content.title: The DHS Shutdown Hits Day 73. Nightmare Airport Lines Could Return Soon.

headline: The Top 5 Analyst Questions From United Airlines’s Q1 Earnings Call
summary: United Airlines’ results for Q1 reflected a period of strong revenue growth but were met with a negative market reaction, as the company contended with sharply higher jet fuel prices and the need for tactical capacity reductions. Management attributed performance to resilient premium and business demand, successful upselling initiatives, and improvements in operational reliability, though cost pressures from fuel, weather disruptions, and flight cancellations weighed on margins. CEO Scott Kirby
